technique: Data staging / collection before exfiltration (archive creation, bulk repository access)

trigger.from_breadth: 4688 archive tooling creating a large/odd-location archive (7z/7za/rar/winrar/tar/Compress-Archive/makecab) in temp/AppData/ProgramData/Public; OR Azure/M365 audit showing mass SharePoint/OneDrive download, mailbox export, or eDiscovery by one principal; OR an IIS data endpoint serving bulk bytes to one client. Chains from a compromise and FEEDS data-exfil-volumetric.

telemetry_required: Azure audit operations (properties_* twins) for SharePoint/OneDrive/mailbox bulk access
telemetry_gap_behavior: SMB-share access auditing (5140/5145) is OFF -> T1039 collection-from-network-share is largely blind; rely on 4688 archive tooling + Azure M365 audit + IIS bulk-download instead, and note the share-access gap. If a byte field is null, sweep the raw message. Absent = KNOWN-GAP, never clean.

method.query: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4688 AND winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security AND message:("7z" OR "7za" OR "rar" OR "winrar" OR "tar " OR "Compress-Archive" OR "makecab")
method.compute: tool, output path + extension, and (where present) size; flag archives written to temp/AppData/ProgramData/Public or named like data/backup/export/dump
method.decision_input: a large archive staged in a temp/odd location by a non-backup process = collection for exfil

method.compute: Azure/M365 audit for one principal: mass FileDownloaded / FileSyncDownloaded (SharePoint/OneDrive), New-MailboxExportRequest, eDiscovery search-export, unusually many distinct documents in the window
method.decision_input: bulk download/export from a repository or mailbox in a short window = data collection (T1213/T1114)

method.compute: tie the staging/collection to one entity; is it off-hours; did it follow a compromise / recon for this entity?
does the same entity show up in data-exfil-volumetric this run (large outbound right after staging)?
method.decision_input: collection -> volumetric egress = confirmed staging-then-exfil -> escalate the chain to CRITICAL

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: staging/collection by an entity that ALSO has a data-exfil-volumetric hit this run (collection -> exfil chain), or that follows a confirmed compromise
decision.HIGH_conf4: large archive staged in temp by a non-backup process, OR bulk repository/mailbox export, with no business explanation
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: archive or bulk-access activity that may be a legitimate workflow - review against the entity baseline
decision.FP_kill: scheduled backup / DB-dump / log-rotation jobs that archive by design; developer build artifacts (CI); known data-export business processes; admin migration tasks in a change window

false_positive_checks: backup / DB-dump / log-archival jobs (confirm via the account baseline + schedule)
known business export/reporting workflows (e.g., scheduled report generation)
Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 as the destination of any follow-on transfer -> cap unless paired with a compromise

output_findings_json.query_template: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:4688 AND message:("7z" OR "rar" OR "Compress-Archive" OR "makecab")
